purpose: Mount Hugging Face Hub repositories and storage buckets as local filesystems via FUSE or NFS, enabling lazy on-demand access to large ML models and datasets without downloading them.

key_modules: src/lib.rs — library root, re-exports core types
src/fuse.rs — FUSE filesystem implementation (page cache invalidation, per-file metadata revalidation)
src/nfs.rs — NFS server filesystem implementation (cross-platform, no root required)
src/hub_api.rs — Hugging Face Hub REST API client for repo/bucket listing and metadata
src/cached_xet_client.rs — cached wrapper around xet-core for content-addressed data fetching
src/xet.rs — xet-core integration layer for efficient file transfers
src/virtual_fs/ — virtual filesystem abstraction layer translating POSIX ops to Hub API calls
patterns: FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) — kernel-level file operation interception
NFS server — userspace NFS for cross-platform compatibility without kernel extensions
Lazy loading / on-demand fetching — only bytes actually read are fetched from remote
Content-addressed storage (via xet-core) — deduplication and efficient transfers
Adaptive prefetch buffering for sequential reads
Metadata TTL caching (10s default staleness window)
Eventual consistency via background polling for remote changes
Daemon pattern for background mount lifecycle management

concepts_for_deepflow: Lazy content fetching: deepflow could lazily fetch spec dependencies or referenced files only when needed during plan/execute, reducing upfront context loading

Dual-backend pattern (FUSE/NFS): deepflow's verify stage could offer pluggable verification backends — lightweight (lint/type-check) vs. heavyweight (browser/integration) — selected per-spec like hf-mount selects FUSE vs NFS

Daemon lifecycle management: deepflow's autonomous mode (df:auto) could adopt a daemon controller pattern — a persistent supervisor process that manages worktree agent lifecycles, health checks, and graceful shutdown
Content-addressed caching (xet-core): deepflow could content-hash spec+code states to cache verification results, skipping re-verification when neither spec nor relevant code has changed
Adaptive prefetch buffering: during df:execute, deepflow could speculatively pre-read files likely needed by upcoming tasks based on dependency analysis, reducing tool-call latency

Metadata TTL with eventual consistency: deepflow's ratchet snapshots could use TTL-based staleness instead of strict freshness, allowing parallel agents to work with slightly stale but consistent views of test state
